𝗠𝗩-𝗦𝗔𝗠𝟯𝗗 | Generating more robust and consistent 3D object reconstructions from multiple input images.  🔥 Super Hot!

♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Generating more robust and consistent 3D object reconstructions from multiple input images.
📅 Created: Nov 2025 • Updated: May 2026

♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Multi-view 3D reconstruction
✅ Extends SAM 3D Objects
✅ Supports single-object generation
✅ Supports multi-object generation
✅ Aims for stable geometry and texture
✅ Aims for scene-level consistency


♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐ 437 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 35 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 11 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

MV-SAM3D is a multi-view 3D reconstruction framework that enhances the SAM 3D Objects model by utilizing multiple viewpoints. It aims to produce more stable and consistent 3D geometry, textures, and scene compositions for both single and multiple objects.

♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/devinli123/MV-SAM3D

✅ Responsive Design

GraphRAG Workbench is an interactive web application for building and visualizing knowledge graphs from documents using Microsoft's GraphRAG framework. It offers advanced AI-powered analysis, natural language querying, and immersive 3D exploration of extracted entities and relationships.

✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Generating exceptionally long and coherent videos by iteratively refining frames and recycling previous errors, enabling applications like continuous storytelling, realistic character animation, and extended visual sequences.
📅 Created: Oct 2025 • Updated: May 2026

✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Infinite-length video generation
✅ Error recycling mechanism
✅ Built on diffusion transformer models
✅ Supports various applications like end-to-end filming, audio-driven talking faces, and dance generation
✅ Available in different versions (SVI 1.0, SVI 2.0, SVI 2.0 Pro) and integrated with ComfyUI workflows


✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Stable Video Infinity (SVI) is a Python-based framework for generating infinitely long videos by recycling errors. It builds upon diffusion transformer models to achieve this, offering advanced capabilities for various video generation tasks.

✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧

🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Managing and running Git hooks efficiently in software development projects, providing a faster and more streamlined alternative to pre-commit.
📅 Created: Oct 2024 • Updated: May 2026

✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱✱

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Single binary with no dependencies
✅ Faster and more efficient than pre-commit
✅ Fully compatible with pre-commit configurations
✅ Built-in support for monorepos
✅ Integration with uv for Python environment management
✅ Improved toolchain installations for multiple languages
✅ Rust-native implementation of common hooks

Prek is a fast, dependency-free Git hook manager written in Rust, serving as a drop-in alternative to pre-commit. It offers improved performance, reduced disk space usage, and enhanced features like monorepo support and integrated Python environment management.

Flowsint is an open-source OSINT graph exploration tool designed for ethical cybersecurity investigations. It visualizes relationships between entities and uses automated enrichers to gather intelligence, storing all data locally for enhanced privacy.
